data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent AC4 ("Sub-op telemetry (links/comments/labels applied) is surfaced, and a `computed > 0 && applied == 0` condition fails loudly") → 2359 AC1 (surfaces links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied in the batch outcome + RECON line = clause 1) + 2359 AC2 (test that computed>0 && applied==0 fails, reproducing the link drop = clause 2). FULLY.
- Parent SC3 ("A silent sub-op no-op … fails a test/canary instead of passing green") → 2359 AC2 (canary fails on the silent no-op). FULLY.
- Contributes to AC7 (2359 AC3: sync semantics unchanged, suite green) and to SC5 (2359 AC4: canary asserts count invariant, not log strings).

DELEGATION — criteria this child does not own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT items (probe wiring → 822a; applier consolidation → 33d0) are consistent with the roster: AC1/AC2/SC1 → 8937 + fe57 + 59ef; AC3/SC2 → 33d0; AC5/SC4 → 822a. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 2359 itself.

4-bucket for this pairing: AC4 + SC3 FULLY (this child); SC5/AC7 contributed; AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/SC1/SC2/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence below).

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; re-cross-checked against the WHOLE roster INCLUDING this child; already tracked across siblings 33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57 — restated here for this pairing's record):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests").
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 2359 AC4 "The canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to THIS story's own canary test; 33d0 AC4, 8937 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57's re-capture doc are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests/fixtures; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 2359 itself for its owned criteria (AC4/SC3 fully covered); the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked across the sibling set.
